findings.description: Initial implementation did not verify campaign.creatorId === user.userId after finding campaign in user's own campaigns.json. A campaign record with a different creatorId could be injected into another user's file, bypassing ownership.
findings.fix_description: Added explicit ownership check `campaign.creatorId !== user.userId` in POST tasks, DELETE tasks, and POST publish endpoints.

findings.description: Path traversal prevention uses SAFE_ID_REGEX /^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]{1,64}$/ which is good. Tested with encoded path traversal, null bytes, and long IDs.
findings.fix_description: All path traversal vectors blocked. Tests added for encoded traversal, null byte, and oversized IDs.

findings.description: Campaign title and description stored without HTML encoding. Not a current XSS risk since data is returned as JSON API response. HTML rendering is in a future task (Task 7).
findings.fix_description: No action for Task 2. HTML encoding will be required when rendering in Task 7 participant page.

findings.description: writeJsonAtomic uses tmp file with predictable naming (pid + timestamp). No security concern since files are in user-owned directories with restricted access.
findings.fix_description: Mirrors the existing webchat.ts pattern. Acceptable for server-side JSON storage.

summary: One major finding (ownership bypass) was fixed. Path traversal protection is solid with regex validation. No current XSS risk in API-only responses. Auth delegation to requireSessionApi is correct.
